mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in recession risks over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 52% accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered cases, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during tightening periods in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index below 2.0 percent year-over-year signals disinflationary pressure that reduces real discount rates, prompting immediate equity buying pressure over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that undermines consumer spending forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in rolling window regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, providing a persistent upward drift in equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5 percent during a post-shock recovery phase confirms manufacturing resilience, prompting immediate equity buying pressure over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 50% in out-of-sample validation.
